# GearBaskets — Camera Gear Guide for Beginner Creators

> GearBaskets provides curated, complete camera gear setups (called "baskets") for beginner content creators. Every basket includes lighting, camera body, lenses, gimbal, and memory card. All prices are shown in live-converted currencies (INR, USD, EUR, GBP, AED).

## What This Site Does

GearBaskets answers the question: "What exact camera gear should I buy as a beginner creator?" with specific, actionable recommendations — not vague lists.

- 12 complete gear setups ("baskets") for different budgets and use cases
- Budget range: ₹1,69,000 to ₹2,92,000 (approximately $1,400 to $2,500 USD)
- All baskets include: LED/strobe lighting, mirrorless camera, lenses (studio + travel), gimbal, SanDisk 512GB SD card
- Live currency conversion for INR, USD, EUR, GBP, AED
- 20 in-depth blog guides on cameras, lenses, lighting, and techniques

## Key Gear Recommendations

### Best Overall Setup (Basket 1)
- Camera: Sony a6700 (~$1,000 USD)
- Studio Lens: Sigma 18-50mm f/2.8 DC DN (~$290 USD)
- Travel Lens: Sony E 18-135mm f/3.5-5.6 OSS (~$300 USD)
- Lights: 2× Godox SL100D 100W LED + 90cm Octa Softbox (~$230 USD)
- Gimbal: DJI RS 3 Mini (~$215 USD)
- Storage: SanDisk 512GB Extreme Pro V90 (~$190 USD)
- Total: ~$2,225 USD

### Best Budget Setup (Basket 9)
- Camera: Canon EOS R50 (~$450 USD)
- Lens: Sigma 18-50mm f/2.8 RF-S Mount (~$370 USD)
- Lights: 2× Godox SL60IID LED (~$150 USD)
- Gimbal: Hohem iSteady MT2 (~$205 USD)
- Storage: SanDisk 512GB V90 (~$190 USD)
- Total: ~$1,365 USD

### Best Studio-Only Setup (Basket 6)
- Camera: Sony a6700 (~$1,000 USD)
- Lens: Sigma 18-50mm f/2.8 (~$290 USD)
- Lights: 2× Godox SL100D LED (~$230 USD)
- Gimbal: DJI RS 3 Mini (~$215 USD)
- Storage: SanDisk 512GB V90 (~$190 USD)
- Total: ~$1,925 USD (no travel lens, studio-focused)

## Use Cases Covered

- Clothing and fashion photography (lehengas, embroidery, fabric)
- Travel vlogging (lightweight dual-lens setups)
- Nature and wildlife photography (long zoom setups)
- Instagram Reels and social media video (vertical gimbal modes)
- Solo creators (AI tracking gimbals)
- Beginners with strict budgets (under $1,400 USD complete setups)

## Cameras Covered

- Sony a6700 (best APS-C overall, 4K 120fps 10-bit)
- Sony ZV-E10 II (same sensor as a6700, ~$400 less, no IBIS)
- Fujifilm X-S20 (best color science, Film Simulations)
- Canon EOS R50 (best autofocus, most beginner-friendly)

## Key Lenses Covered

- Sigma 18-50mm f/2.8 DC DN (Sony E, Fuji X, Canon RF-S mounts) — primary studio lens
- Sony E 18-135mm f/3.5-5.6 OSS — travel and nature zoom
- Sony E 70-350mm f/4.5-6.3 G OSS — wildlife telephoto (525mm equivalent)
- Fujifilm XF 18-120mm f/4 PZ — power zoom for Fuji users

## Memory Card Recommendation

SanDisk 512GB Extreme Pro V90 (UHS-II, 300MB/s write) — required for Sony a6700 4K 120fps 10-bit recording. Compatible with: Sony a6700, Sony ZV-E10 II, Fujifilm X-S20, Canon EOS R50.

19. Camera gear upgrade guide
20. Canon R50 vs Sony a6700 comparison

## Source Quality

All gear recommendations are based on real-world testing data and verified specifications. Prices are estimates for the Indian market (INR) with live conversion to USD, EUR, GBP, AED. Users should verify current prices before purchasing.
